title: phlegmariurus sieboldii
intro: Phlegmariurus sieboldii, also known as Siebold's climbing forked polypody or climbing polypody, is a species of creeping fern in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is a small, perennial plant that grows on trees and rocks, and has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its medicinal properties. Phlegmariurus sieboldii is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and it is also said to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus sieboldii is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
